summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting public hearing on zoning case ZC-2021-008, a request by Grand Communities (developer for Fischer Homes) to rezone 39.48 acres on Kerbert Road from R2 to PDR for a 117-lot single-family residential subdivision. After extensive staff presentation, applicant testimony, and public comment raising concerns about traffic, road width, drainage, school capacity, housing density, and the secondary emergency access road, the board unanimously denied the application because the proposed 1,200 square foot minimum home size did not meet the township's 1,400 square foot zoning requirement.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed

Motion to deny zoning case ZC-2021-008 (zone change from R2 to PDR for 117-lot subdivision on Kerbert Road) due to failure to meet the 1,400 square foot minimum living space zoning requirement - passed unanimously (Pazos yes, Allen yes, Hodges yes)
Motion to adjourn the special meeting at 7:36 PM - passed
action_items: Director Holbrook to notify applicant Grand Communities of the board's denial decision in accordance with applicable legal authority
key_amounts: 1,200 square feet proposed minimum home size (did not meet 1,400 sq ft requirement)
1,400 square feet minimum required by zoning resolution
1,483 square feet average existing home size on Kerbert Road
2.96 dwelling units per acre (3 DUA maximum)
Price range of high $270,000s to $320,000s for proposed homes
$1,200 per year farming lease income Goshen Junior Sports would lose on 16 acres
Estimated 800 cars per day from proposed development
18 feet current road width on Kerbert Road
notable_topics: Zoning case ZC-2021-008: R2 to PDR zone change request for 117-lot subdivision on Kerbert Road
Minimum home square footage requirement (1,200 proposed vs 1,400 required)
Kerbert Road traffic, width, and safety concerns
Intersection safety at Kerbert Road and State Route 132 (line of sight issues)
Secondary emergency access road design (structural grass pavers vs public roadway)
Drainage and stormwater retention concerns for neighboring properties
Impact on Heisler Park/Goshen Junior Sports operations and traffic
County water line extension and tap fees for existing residents
School district capacity concerns with additional development
Community desire for larger, higher-end homes rather than starter/entry-level homes
Cumulative traffic impact with other proposed developments (Short 48 area)
Fire code requirements for two means of ingress/egress for developments over 30 homes
attendees: Director Ward Holbrook (Planning and Zoning Director)
Kirk Ritter (Grand Communities, developer for Fischer Homes)
Etta Reed (Bayer and Becker, engineer/surveyor/landscape architect for Grand Communities)
Joe Hundley (Heisler Park/Goshen Junior Sports board representative, 1502 Wynwood Drive)
notes: The board unanimously denied the zoning change primarily on the technical grounds that the proposed 1,200 sq ft minimum home size did not meet the 1,400 sq ft requirement, though trustees also expressed broader concerns about the development being too dense and consisting of starter homes rather than the larger homes they want for Goshen. All three trustees expressed desire for Fisher Homes to return with larger, more upscale homes. The board noted they are pro-property-owner and pro-growth but want the right type of development. A regular board meeting was scheduled to follow after a 15-20 minute break.
